directives.title: Build RFQ send tracker and status dashboard
directives.task: Create tools/rfq-generator/rfq_tracker.py that manages the full RFQ lifecycle. It must: (1) read all .txt files from tools/rfq-generator/drafts/, (2) maintain a tools/rfq-generator/rfq_status.json file tracking each RFQ with fields: contact, company, category, est_value_usd, status (DRAFTED|SENT|RESPONDED|AWARDED|DECLINED), sent_date, response_date, quoted_price, notes, (3) produce tools/rfq-generator/rfq_status_report.md showing pipeline summary, total value in each status bucket, and next actions. Pre-populate rfq_status.json with Baker Hughes VIB_MON as RESPONDED (quoted $340K on Apr 10 2026) and all 6 drafted RFQs as DRAFTED.
directives.context: 6 RFQ drafts exist in tools/rfq-generator/drafts/. Baker Hughes already responded at $340K. The tracker needs to show total pipeline value, conversion rate, and next actions clearly.

directives.title: Build supplier intelligence enrichment for top 10 strategic suppliers
directives.task: Create tools/supplier-intelligence/enrich_suppliers.py and produce tools/supplier-intelligence/supplier_profiles.md with intelligence profiles for the top 10 strategic suppliers: GE Vernova, Siemens Energy, ABB Power, Emerson (Power & Water), Donaldson Company, Baker Hughes (TPS), CECO Environmental, Eaton Electrical, Flowserve, and EthosEnergy. For each supplier include: company overview, relevant product lines for W251 BOP, key decision maker contacts known, estimated lead times, known W251/frame GT references, and recommended outreach approach. Use only information available in the repo and general knowledge — no external API calls required.
directives.context: Project Jupiter 50MW W251B8 BOP. These are the top-tier suppliers across STRATEGIC spend categories. Profiles should be concise but actionable — 1 page per supplier.

directives.title: Build automated BOP cost model Excel-equivalent CSV with scenario analysis
directives.task: Create tools/pricing-discovery/bop_cost_model.py that generates tools/pricing-discovery/outputs/bop_cost_model.csv with full scenario analysis. The CSV must have columns: category, category_code, bom_low, bom_mid, bom_high, rfq_quoted, rfq_variance_pct, confidence, spend_tier, preferred_supplier, avoid_supplier, scenario_optimistic, scenario_base, scenario_pessimistic. Populate rfq_quoted for VIB_MON with 340000. Compute scenario columns as: optimistic = bom_low * 0.95, base = bom_mid, pessimistic = bom_high * 1.10. Add a summary row with totals. Also produce outputs/bop_scenario_summary.md with a 3-scenario comparison table.
directives.context: BOM data is in tools/pricing-discovery/bom_library.py. Baker Hughes quoted $340K for VIB_MON. All other categories at BOM estimate only. Scenarios: optimistic ($7.85M), base ($9.27M), pessimistic ($10.6M).

directives.title: Build contact outreach sequence generator
directives.task: Create tools/contact-verifier/outreach_sequences.py that generates personalized 3-touch outreach sequences for the top 8 W251 priority contacts. For each contact produce: Touch 1 (initial RFQ intro, reference Project Jupiter), Touch 2 (follow-up 5 days later, add urgency — Q3 2026 award target), Touch 3 (final follow-up 10 days after Touch 1, offer alternative contact or pivot). Output to tools/contact-verifier/outreach_sequences/ as individual .md files per contact. Contacts: Lorenzo Simonelli (BH CEO), Bob Yeager (Emerson), Tod Carpenter (Donaldson), Michael Wynblatt (Donaldson), Rod Christie (BH EVP), Harrison K (Amerex), Neil Ashford (Turbotect), Alberto Malandra (EthosEnergy Italia).
directives.context: Sequences should be professional C-suite appropriate. Sign as Greg Buchanan, CEO Trans World Power. Reference W251B8, Project Jupiter, Santa Teresa NM, Oracle/OpenAI campus. EthosEnergy sequence should reference the MOU signed March 13 2026 and Todd Dunlop as technical contact.
